title: Fawn Proof Artifact
description: Machine-readable artifact emitted by Lean proof extraction. Compilation of the extraction module verifies all listed theorems. Decidable propositions are additionally evaluated at runtime.

status.description: verified means all theorems typechecked and all evaluations completed.

comparabilityObligationsSha256.description: SHA-256 of config/comparability-obligations.json that generated the Lean comparability contract.

category.description: Verification tier. tautological: correct by construction or definitional (nothing to verify). comptime_verified: finite-enum property verifiable by Zig comptime exhaustion. lean_verified: quantified over unbounded domains (Lean is necessary). lean_fixture: specific test case exercising a lean_verified theorem. lean_required: theorem that requires Lean proof and blocks release gates.

evaluatedConditions.description: Decidable propositions evaluated at extraction time. Keys are dotted condition identifiers, values are booleans or integers.

eliminationTargets.description: Mapping from proven theorems to runtime branches that can be eliminated.

theorem.description: Lean theorem name that proves the condition.

condition.description: Human-readable description of the proven invariant.

runtimePath.description: Zig source path and function where the runtime branch can be eliminated.

boundsEliminations.description: Proven bounds conditions that allow the robustness transform to elide clamps. Each entry maps a Lean theorem to an index access pattern that is provably safe under stated preconditions.

theorem.description: Lean theorem name proving the bounds condition.

category.description: Verification tier for this bounds elimination theorem.

pattern.description: Index access pattern matched by the robustness transform.

precondition.description: Host-side dispatch precondition required for the proof to apply.

eliminates.description: The clamp expression that becomes a no-op under the precondition.

runtimePath.description: Zig source path and function where the clamp is elided.
